welcome:
Welcome back, Pricila!

Onboarding

Last day in Sydney! Before you fly home, you want a souvenir. This week: how to ask prices, say something is too expensive, and bargain for a discount. Review these emergency phrases before class.

How much is this?
That is too expensive.
Can you give me a discount?
Do you take cash?
audit:
<!--
SCENARIO FIT — Aula 10
Can-do: "I can ask prices, say if something is too expensive, bargain for a discount, and buy a souvenir."
Gramatica-alvo: quantifiers (How much is/are...?, how many), prices, too + adjective, adjective + enough / not + adj + enough; bargaining (Can you give me a discount? / I will take it).
Vocab-alvo: market, stall, discount, cash, change, bargain, size, souvenir.
Cenario escolhido: mercado de rua em Sydney (Pricila, no ultimo dia da viagem, compra um souvenir e pechincha o preco).
Por que elicita o alvo: comprar e pechinchar OBRIGA o aluno a perguntar precos (How much...?), reagir (too expensive / not cheap enough) e negociar (Can you give me a discount?). >70% dos itens-alvo (8 vocab + too/enough + price questions) sao naturalmente elicitados. Habitat natural do focoLinguistico.

CONTINUIDADE — Aula 10
Itens novos desta aula: market, stall, discount, cash, change, bargain, size, souvenir; quantifiers/prices + too/enough + bargaining.
Itens revisados (de aulas anteriores): "Could we have the bill?" e vocab de restaurante (Aula 9); pedidos educados Could/Would (Aulas 6-7); pagar/dinheiro como ponte.
Callback no warm-up (slide 2): retoma ATIVAMENTE 2 itens da Aula 9 — (1) o aluno pede a conta ("Could we have the bill?") e (2) usa "bill/waiter" para fazer a ponte: pagou o jantar, saiu e achou um mercado para comprar souvenirs.
NB: nenhum item-alvo novo repete vocabulario ja ensinado nas Aulas 1-9. Fecha o bloco de sobrevivencia em viagem (aeroporto -> hotel -> transporte -> restaurante -> compras).
-->

challenges.prompt: Ask the price of a scarf.
Ask for a different size and if they take cash.
challenges.answer: "Do you have a smaller size? Do you take cash?"

challenges.prompt: Ask the price of two items together.

challenges.answer: "It is a good price. I will take it."
